description: Add a text-selection-based comment UI to the plan-harness HTML dashboard. Reviewers highlight text in any rendered plan document and leave comments Word-style, stored as JSONL on the host filesystem and attributed to the logged-in reviewer. Requires stable deterministic section IDs in generated HTML as a prerequisite. Establishes the built-in collaboration layer that removes dependence on external tools.

patterns: MCP server over stdio — stdout reserved for JSON-RPC, logs to stderr
Pre-built single-file dist bundle shipped in repo; end users never npm install
Self-contained HTML output with inline CSS/JS, CSS custom-prop theming
Plans live in the target repo (not in this plugin repo) under plans/<scenario>/
Scenario state is read from filesystem; no database
conventions: MCP logging goes to stderr via console.error
Templates use CSS variables and [data-theme] attribute for dark/light
Start.js launches from dist/index.js (no runtime install)
Cross-platform: no Windows-only code, path.sep / pathToFileURL everywhere
Public plugin — no Microsoft/CodeBlend-tied tooling or co-author attribution
Write concise code — minimal comments, default to no comments
Loopback requests bypass auth; only remote (tunneled) viewers need to log in

selfDevelopment.note: This scenario is planning plan-harness itself. The plugin being edited IS the plugin running the planning. This creates constraints that downstream agents must respect.
constraints: Claude Code loads the plugin from a per-user cache (~/.claude/plugins/cache/canwa-claude-plugins/plan-harness/<version>/), not from this repo directly. Edits to repo source have no effect until sync'd.
Dev loop: edit src → `npm run dev` (builds dist + syncs to cache) → restart Claude Code.
The MCP server is a long-running stdio process; new bundles only load at Claude Code startup.
Skill/prompt edits (markdown-only) still need `npm run sync` + CC restart to take effect.
Generated plan documents land in <repoRoot>/plans/built-in-comment-ui/ (i.e., inside this same repo). Decide per-scenario whether to commit them.
HTML templates currently live in local-proxy/src/templates/base.js — changes there are what this scenario implements. After implementing, future plan runs will benefit (self-improving loop).

scripts.npm run sync: copy working copy into plugin cache

relevantSections: Feature Backlog > Now > Stable section IDs
Feature Backlog > High leverage > Text-selection comment UI
designDecisions: HTML remains source of truth (not markdown) — charts/images/interactivity don't survive markdown
Built-in comment UI + external adapter exports (not PR-review-of-plans)
Comments anchor via W3C TextQuoteSelector (exact + prefix + suffix) for regen resilience
Section IDs are deterministic hash of title + parent path — needed for both anchoring and re-anchoring after regen
Reviewer identity set on login (password + name), attached server-side to comments (never client-supplied)
Rejected better-auth — DB-backed, overkill for ephemeral per-share password. Built minimal auth.js (~160 LoC)
openQuestions: Real-time sync across simultaneous reviewers (polling vs WebSocket)?
Resolve semantics: collapse thread or just flag?
Author badge counts on scenario card?
Can agent personas (architect/PM) respond to comments in review cycle?

recentWork.authOverhaul: Commit 63b3bbc hardened plan_share auth: no URL token, HMAC-signed sessions, timing-safe compare, rate limiting, rolling TTL, loopback bypass. Login form captures reviewer name. /api/me endpoint returns authenticated reviewer's name.
recentWork.distBundle: esbuild bundle at plan-harness/local-proxy/dist/index.js; committed; start.js imports directly.
recentWork.stableSectionIds: NOT yet implemented — the HTML templates in local-proxy/src/templates/base.js still emit non-deterministic section IDs. This is the prerequisite to address first in this scenario.
